signals.reasoning: Keep managing the live MON short for now. It is back in profit, still retaining about two-thirds of peak P&L, and the warning is no longer urgent, so this is a manage-not-force-close situation.
BTC is firm but still lacks enough directional edge to outrank the more actionable alt setups.
ETH is constructive but does not offer a clean enough setup to justify a fresh trade here.
SOL is one of the better long candidates because the board is bullish and funding remains negative enough to support a squeeze-style continuation.
STBL remains a clean bearish fade because funding is very positive, crowding is high, and the market still looks fragile on the upside.
MAVIA also screens as a fade candidate because funding is positive, crowding is high, and the market quality is weak enough that upside continuation is suspect.
HMSTR is too illiquid and noisy to be a serious setup despite mild momentum.
HEMI has some constructive bias, but not enough trade quality to justify action.
USTC is extremely thin and low-quality, so it stays off the actionable list.
DASH remains a valid squeeze-style long because mechanics are still strong and funding is supportive for continuation after the veto cleared.
